title: gambelia rupicola
intro: Gambelia rupicola, also known as rocky mountain bee plant, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant contains bioactive compounds like iridoid glycosides, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Gambelia rupicola has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Gambelia rupicola can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
